3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"os"
6		"strings"
7	
8		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/config"

12	
13	// resolveTsnetAuthKey applies auth key precedence:
14	// TS_AUTHKEY env var > --tsnet-auth-key CLI flag > config file auth_key.
15	// The env var is preferred because CLI flags are visible in ps/history.
16	func resolveTsnetAuthKey(envKey, flagKey, configKey string) string {
17		if envKey != "" {
18			return envKey
19		}
20		if flagKey != "" {
21			return flagKey
22		}
23		return configKey
24	}
25	
26	// defaultTsnetHostname returns "ddx-<short-hostname>" for the current host,
27	// falling back to "ddx" when the hostname is unavailable or empty.
28	func defaultTsnetHostname() string {
29		h, err := os.Hostname()
30		if err != nil || h == "" {
31			return "ddx"
32		}
33		// Trim any trailing domain so the ts-net name stays short and stable.
34		if i := strings.Index(h, "."); i >= 0 {
35			h = h[:i]
36		}
37		return "ddx-" + h
38	}
39	
40	func (f *CommandFactory) newServerCommand() *cobra.Command {
41		var port int
42		var addr string
43		var tlsCert string
44		var tlsKey string
45		var tsnetEnabled bool
46		var tsnetHostname string
47		var tsnetAuthKey string
48	
49		cmd := &cobra.Command{
50			Use:   "server",
51			Short: "Start the DDx HTTP and MCP server",
52			Long: `Start the DDx server exposing documents, beads, document graph,
53	and agent session logs over HTTP REST and MCP endpoints.
54	
55	HTTP API:
56	  GET /api/health              Liveness check
57	  GET /api/ready               Readiness check with dependency status
58	  GET /api/documents           List library documents (?type=)
59	  GET /api/documents/:path     Read a document
60	  GET /api/search?q=           Full-text search across documents
61	  GET /api/personas/:role      Resolve persona for a role
62	  GET /api/beads               List beads (?status=&label=)
63	  GET /api/beads/:id           Show a specific bead
64	  GET /api/beads/ready         List ready beads
65	  GET /api/beads/blocked       List blocked beads
66	  GET /api/beads/status        Bead summary counts
67	  GET /api/beads/dep/tree/:id  Dependency tree for a bead
68	  GET /api/docs/graph          Document dependency graph
69	  GET /api/docs/stale          Stale documents
70	  GET /api/docs/:id            Document metadata and staleness
71	  GET /api/docs/:id/deps       Upstream dependencies
72	  GET /api/docs/:id/dependents Downstream dependents
73	  GET /api/agent/sessions      List agent sessions (?harness=&since=)
74	  GET /api/agent/sessions/:id  Session detail
75	  GET /api/metrics/summary     Process metrics summary (?since=)
76	  GET /api/metrics/cost        Process metrics cost (?bead=&feature=&since=)
77	  GET /api/metrics/cycle-time  Process metrics cycle time (?since=)
78	  GET /api/metrics/rework      Process metrics rework (?since=)
79	
80	MCP (POST /mcp):
81	  ddx_list_documents           List library documents
82	  ddx_read_document            Read a document
83	  ddx_search                   Full-text search
84	  ddx_resolve_persona          Resolve persona for a role
85	  ddx_list_beads               List beads
86	  ddx_show_bead                Show a specific bead
87	  ddx_bead_ready               List ready beads
88	  ddx_bead_status              Bead summary counts
89	  ddx_doc_graph                Document dependency graph
90	  ddx_doc_stale                Stale documents
91	  ddx_doc_show                 Document metadata
92	  ddx_doc_deps                 Upstream dependencies
93	  ddx_agent_sessions           List agent sessions`,
94			RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
95				listenAddr := fmt.Sprintf("%s:%d", addr, port)
96				fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "DDx server listening on https://%s\n", listenAddr)
97				srv := server.New(listenAddr, f.WorkingDir)
98	
99				// Build tsnet config. tsnet is on by default — flags override config,
100				// and --tsnet=false disables it. Hostname defaults to ddx-<hostname>.
101				tc := &config.TsnetConfig{Enabled: true}
102				if cfg, err := config.LoadWithWorkingDir(f.WorkingDir); err == nil && cfg.Server != nil && cfg.Server.Tsnet != nil {
103					*tc = *cfg.Server.Tsnet
104				}
105				if cmd.Flags().Changed("tsnet") {
106					tc.Enabled = tsnetEnabled
107				}
108				if tsnetHostname != "" {
109					tc.Hostname = tsnetHostname
110				}
111				if tc.Hostname == "" {
112					tc.Hostname = defaultTsnetHostname()
113				}
114				// Prefer TS_AUTHKEY env var; CLI flag is a fallback (secrets on CLI are visible in ps/history)
115				tc.AuthKey = resolveTsnetAuthKey(os.Getenv("TS_AUTHKEY"), tsnetAuthKey, tc.AuthKey)
116				if tc.Enabled {
117					srv.TsnetConfig = tc
118					fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "DDx ts-net enabled (hostname: %s)\n", tc.Hostname)
119				}
120	
121				return srv.ListenAndServeTLS(tlsCert, tlsKey)
122			},
123		}
124	
125		cmd.Flags().IntVarP(&port, "port", "p", 7743, "Port to listen on")
126		cmd.Flags().StringVar(&addr, "addr", "127.0.0.1", "Address to bind to")
127		cmd.Flags().StringVar(&tlsCert, "tls-cert", "", "TLS certificate file (PEM); auto-generates self-signed cert if omitted")
128		cmd.Flags().StringVar(&tlsKey, "tls-key", "", "TLS private key file (PEM); auto-generates self-signed key if omitted")
129		cmd.Flags().BoolVar(&tsnetEnabled, "tsnet", true, "Enable Tailscale ts-net listener (use --tsnet=false to disable; see ADR-006)")
130		cmd.Flags().StringVar(&tsnetHostname, "tsnet-hostname", "", "Tailscale hostname (default: ddx-<hostname>)")
131		cmd.Flags().StringVar(&tsnetAuthKey, "tsnet-auth-key", "", "Tailscale auth key for headless/CI use (SECURITY: visible in ps/history; prefer TS_AUTHKEY env var)")
132	
133		// Worker management
134		cmd.AddCommand(f.newServerWorkersCommand())
135	
136		// State file utilities (prune, …)
137		cmd.AddCommand(f.newServerStateCommand())
138	
139		// Service management (systemd on Linux, launchd on macOS)
140		cmd.AddCommand(f.newServerInstallCommand())
141		cmd.AddCommand(f.newServerUninstallCommand())
142		cmd.AddCommand(f.newServerStartCommand())
143		cmd.AddCommand(f.newServerStopCommand())
144		cmd.AddCommand(f.newServerStatusCommand())
145	
146		return cmd
147	}

{"acceptance":"- [ ] WorkerManager starts a supervisor goroutine on first worker launch
- [ ] Watchdog reaps workers past `WatchdogDeadline` with no phase transition in `StallDeadline`
- [ ] SIGTERM→SIGKILL escalation works on an intentionally-wedged test worker (unit test with a goroutine that ignores ctx)
- [ ] Reaped worker's bead claim is released (tracker status returns to open or claim-expired)
- [ ] `bead.reaped` event recorded on tracker with reason="watchdog" and duration
- [ ] Watchdog deadlines are configurable via ddx server config","claimed-at":"2026-04-18T05:48:25Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1833233","closing_commit_sha":"13cef95535aea82c1f00dd596704f141ed131af7","created_at":"2026-04-18T04:43:00.09109294Z","description":"## Residual risk after RC1–RC4 from ddx-0a651925

Even with context propagation (RC1), wall-clock bounds (RC2), tightened breakers (RC3), and provider deadlines (RC4), a goroutine-level deadlock or panic-swallowed handler could still leave a worker alive past any reasonable bound. Today, `server.WorkerManager.Stop` (workers.go:648) only calls `handle.cancel()` — it has no escalation to OS-level signal and no autonomous trigger. ddx-b808df39 adds the *operator* tool; this bead adds the *autonomous* watchdog.

## Scope

1. WorkerManager runs a supervisor goroutine that, every minute, inspects each `workerHandle`.
2. If `time.Since(record.StartedAt) > WatchdogDeadline` (default e.g. 6h) AND `record.CurrentAttempt` has not transitioned in `StallDeadline` (default e.g. 1h), escalate:
   - Send SIGTERM to the worker's process group (requires surfacing PID, aligned with ddx-b808df39).
   - After 30s grace, send SIGKILL.
   - Mark record.State = "reaped"; update claimed bead state so the claim is not leaked.
3. Emit a `bead.reaped` event on the tracker with the full timeline so post-mortems are easy.

## Non-goals

- Per-bead runtime tuning
...

cli/internal/git/git.go:23:func FindProjectRoot(startDir string) string {

20	// ddx always operates from the repository root regardless of the caller's
21	// working directory. Without this, running `ddx` from a subdirectory that
22	// contains its own `.ddx/` folder would silently use the wrong workspace.
23	func FindProjectRoot(startDir string) string {
24		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Second)
25		defer cancel()
26	
27		cmd := Command(ctx, startDir, "rev-parse", "--show-toplevel")
28		out, err := cmd.Output()
29		if err != nil {
30			// Not in a git repo — fall back to the original directory.
31			return startDir
32		}
33		root := strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
34		if root == "" {
35			return startDir
36		}
37		return root
38	}
39	
40	// FindNearestDDxWorkspace walks up from startDir to find the nearest ancestor
41	// inside the current git repository that contains a .ddx workspace.
42	//
43	// When startDir is inside a linked git worktree (e.g. an execute-bead
44	// isolated worktree under .ddx/.execute-bead-wt-* or a worktrunk sibling
45	// like repo.feature-branch/), it resolves to the PRIMARY worktree's .ddx/
46	// first, not the linked worktree's own .ddx/. This is critical because:
47	//
48	//   - bead store mutations must land in the canonical project bead queue,
49	//     not in an isolated worktree's private snapshot that will be discarded
50	//   - the primary worktree is the operator's source of truth; linked
51	//     worktrees are ephemeral execution contexts
52	//
53	// If the primary worktree has no .ddx/ (or we aren't in a linked worktree),
54	// it falls back to walking up from startDir within the current git
55	// repository.
56	//
57	// Returns an empty string if no .ddx/ is found.

input.content:
# Playwright e2e fixture workspace

This directory provides a self-contained DDx workspace that the default
Playwright harness copies into a temp directory before booting the Go DDx
server. The fixture isolates e2e runs from the developer's `$HOME` and from the
repository's live `.ddx/` state.

Contents:

- `.ddx/config.yaml` — minimal DDx config pointing at the local plugin library.
- `.ddx/beads.jsonl` — open, closed, and blocked beads so the bead API endpoints
  return non-empty data.
- `.ddx/plugins/ddx/` — minimal personas, prompts, and templates so the document
  and persona endpoints have something to list.
- `docs/` — small docs library for the document graph endpoint.

The fixture is read-only; the harness copies it to a `mktemp -d` workspace at
boot so test runs do not mutate these files. Do not reference `$HOME` or `~/`
from anything in this directory.

input.content:
# Playwright e2e fixture workspace

This directory provides a self-contained DDx workspace that the default
Playwright harness copies into a temp directory before booting the Go DDx
server. The fixture isolates e2e runs from the developer's home directory and
from the repository's live `.ddx/` state.

Contents:

- `.ddx/config.yaml` — minimal DDx config pointing at the local plugin library.
- `.ddx/beads.jsonl` — open, closed, and blocked beads so the bead API endpoints
  return non-empty data.
- `.ddx/plugins/ddx/` — minimal personas, prompts, and templates so the document
  and persona endpoints have something to list.
- `docs/` — small docs library for the document graph endpoint.

The fixture is read-only; the harness copies it to a `mktemp -d` workspace at
boot so test runs do not mutate these files. Do not reference user-home paths
from anything in this directory.

git commit -m "test(frontend): switch default Playwright harness to Go-server fixture [ddx-fc6e5b14]

The default Playwright harness now copies e2e/fixtures/ into a temp
workspace and boots the embedded Go DDx server against it, so the API
tests in TC-008 (and other backend-dependent specs) hit real /api/*
endpoints instead of the static SvelteKit preview.

The fixture directory ships minimal .ddx/config.yaml, .ddx/beads.jsonl,
plugin library content, and a docs/ tree, all isolated from the
developer's home directory and the repository's live .ddx/ state."

data.text: Committed `e38318c6`. The default Playwright harness now copies `e2e/fixtures/` into a temp workspace, builds the ddx CLI from the cli module, and boots `ddx server --tsnet=false` on `127.0.0.1:4174` against that isolated workspace. All 7 TC-008 tests pass (6 API tests in `app.spec.ts` + 1 navigation test). All four AC commands verified green.
